assets.abstract: Nasal breathing can entrain fast oscillations in the prefrontal cortex and limbic systems, as experiments with rodents and intracranial EEG recordings in patients have shown. Recently, it was demonstrated that the activity of the amygdala and hippocampus can also be studied non-invasively, using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). When simultaneously recording BOLD signals, respiration and cardiac RR interval (RRI) time courses, and applying a multivariate autoregressive (MVAR) model c
